author: sentientcake
summary: Limit each segment to 2 atomic actions
raw_text: Going forward, each segment is limited to a maximum of two atomic actions.

author: jayric5
raw_text: not atomic actions I am talking about this 🚀 Audit Results & Key Reminders Hello Team, We’ve completed another audit round - overall, great work. You can find your specific results via platform notifications or the Feedback section. Please review these critical focus areas to maintain high-quality data: 1. "No Action" vs. "Hold" Hold: Use when the ego is holding a task-relevant object, even if stationary. No Action: Strictly for when hands touch nothing or are doing something unrelated to the task. Never use "No Action" while the subject is holding a task-relevant object. 2. Optimal Segment Length Granularity: While segments can be long, the best practice is to describe only 1–2 atomic actions per segment. ➡️ **Accuracy: Breaking episodes into smaller segments (ideally 3–10 words) minimizes the risk of missing actions or hallucinating steps.**✍️ Constraint: A single segment must never exceed 20 words. 3. Labeling "Adjust" Definition: "Adjust" refers to a small corrective change in an object's position or orientation. Requirement: If there is a clear repositioning of an object in the ego's hand, it must be labeled to ensure the action is captured. Performance Update: Going forward, we are implementing a bonus for high audit accuracy and a small penalty for low accuracy or repeated major mistakes. Keep up the momentum!

author: mbuan1823
summary: Limit each segment to 2 atomic actions
raw_text: i think ATLAS needs to revised their Playbook... because there it indicates a SEGMENT (starts when hand engage and ends when hand disengage or goal changes) but it is no longer possible because of the maximum of 2 atomic actions per segment.. and now this HOLD thing... please revised so labelers will have a proof of NEW rules implemented.

author: 66e8a0b0d666b9bcf4e26593
summary: Possible max segment duration update requires review
raw_text: # 📢 Segmenting Update — New Guidelines Hey @everyone! We're rolling out some changes to how segmenting works. Here's what's changing: - Shorter segments are the new standard! - The max segment duration is now: **10 seconds**, with **2–5 seconds being the sweet spot** - Within each segment, label all actions and pauses - We want as much granularity as possible, dense is better! **Your job is getting easier**. We're improving our auto-labels, so going forward you'll mostly be correcting labels rather than creating them from scratch. No penalties for past work. These are new guidelines and you won't be penalized for anything done before this change! We will be holding daily workshops to go over these changes to help you all through the transition. Happy labeling! Does this mean we should not marge a segment that os more than 10secs? Any clarification on this please
